name: DOGE Retest Hold De-Risk v1
thesis: DOGE 4h break impulses can work better after a retest-and-hold than on the initial expansion bar. The edge should come from delayed continuation entries with quick time-based de-risk if expansion fails to appear promptly.
edge_mechanism: retest hold continuation with time-based de-risking
invalidation_condition: Abort if retest logic still collapses into sparse no-trade grammar, contradictory confirmation overlap, or fails to produce post-cost edge.

pre_submit_reasoning.market_behavior_targeted: Breakout continuation only after the broken level is revisited and holds instead of failing immediately.
pre_submit_reasoning.why_edge_should_exist: Delayed entry after retest can filter false first-break moves while still allowing participation in genuine follow-through bursts.
pre_submit_reasoning.asset_timeframe_fit: DOGE 4h often moves in impulse-reset cycles where retest logic is more realistic than perfect first-break timing.
pre_submit_reasoning.likely_failure_mode: If breakouts either fail immediately or run away without retesting, entries may still be underrepresented.
pre_submit_reasoning.first_refinement_path: If participation exists but density is weak, widen retest proximity before changing time-based de-risk rules.

density_sanity_check.contradictory_filters: none obvious; retest and persistence filters align with expansion context
density_sanity_check.event_sparsity: managed by broad retest proximity and modest ADX floor

long: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.25 * ATR_14
short: Time-based de-risk after 2 bars if unrealized gain < 0.25 * ATR_14
